tweets.title: France welcomes Iran peace deal to be signed on Friday:
tweets.content:
France welcomes Iran peace deal to be signed on Friday:

Emmanuel Macron (@EmmanuelMacron)

Je salue l’accord conclu entre les États-Unis et l’Iran, fruit d’un effort diplomatique auquel ont contribué plusieurs partenaires. J’appelle à sa mise en œuvre rapide et complète par tous les belligérants.

Cet accord doit permettre la réouverture urgente et inconditionnelle du détroit d’Ormuz, que la mission internationale mise en place avec le Royaume-Uni est prête à accompagner. Les moyens sont en place et prêts à être engagés. La reprise du trafic maritime, sans restriction ni péage, est une condition indispensable à la stabilité régionale et à l’économie mondiale.

Cet accord ouvre également la voie à une négociation globale au service de la paix et de la sécurité de tous au Moyen-Orient. Celle-ci devra permettre de répondre aux préoccupations liées aux programmes nucléaire et balistique de l’Iran, ainsi qu’à sa politique de déstabilisation régionale. C’est ainsi que nous pourrons bâtir une paix solide et durable. La France est disposée à y prendre toute sa part, aux côtés de ses partenaires.

Elle continuera également à apporter son plein soutien aux efforts déterminés des autorités libanaises pour restaurer la souveraineté de l’État, qui est le seul à même d’assurer la stabilité et l’intégrité territoriale du Liban et de répondre aux besoins des populations. Un cessez-le-feu robuste et durable est pour cela indispensable.

— https://nitter.net/EmmanuelMacron/status/2066291258721468594#m

tweets.title:
RT by @alpha_defense: Everytime a discussion on India’s journey in frontier tech comes, the opinion is always divided on which way India should go. The American way(state largesse funded SOTA basic research in academia and industry picking up the baton and then running the next 1000 miles with its cash flows) or China way(state funded, state directed largesse and state controlled but pvt sector executed). 

India has structural problems in pursuing either of the way that we are not ready to discuss head on but beat around with either “look at SpaceX Google, Indian pvt sector should pick up” or “look at China, all are govt funded efforts under writing the risk”. In the end we come up with a lame ass namesake answer of frugal engineering that never really solves for the mission at hand but just make does with the status quo and tries to produce whatever we can. 

Because the constraint here is about solving for talent, resources and capital with the latter taking care of the former two of enough of it is thrown at the problems. Who should solve for the capital problem in India’s case today if India’s stated ambition today is sovereignty and strategic autonomy? 

India needs a new compact and that needs to look at India from first principles, Indian state, public and the industry shedding certain aspects and pursuing newer mechanisms. 

State banks giving zero interest loans? Govt establishing sovereign fund with full risk appetite? Govt directing CSR funds for fundamental research? Tax holidays? India’s equivalent of thousand talents program? India’s equivalent of annually multi billion $$ NSF? Special Research Zones along lines of GIFT city? 

More deeper stuff like allowing freer capital flows, getting rid of reservation and permanent cadre in key STEM research orgs and bringing hire and fire with lateral entries, pay structure changes, a stated goal to  achieve a 3X GOI spending on R&D from current levels in a PPP mode so that GOI ₹₹ acts as catalyst for pvt sector to put in their share and above this bringing restrictions on foreign tech use at strategic orgs. 

Ppl who try to dampen this with demanding expectations commensurate with India’s PCI, cost of sovereignty doesn’t track PCI. It costs whatever it takes to have strategic autonomy. Those expecting sovereign capabilities at frugal costs and without structural reforms are essentially demanding free lunch for an unserious player.

Ministry of Defence, Government of India (@SpokespersonMoD)

The Government has appointed Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth, PVSM, UYSM, AVSM, presently serving as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff, as the next Chief of the Army Staff in the substantive rank of General with effect from the afternoon of 30 June 2026.

An alumnus of the National Defence Academy and an Armoured Corps officer, Lt Gen Seth brings with him nearly four decades of distinguished service, marked by extensive command, staff and strategic appointments across diverse operational and institutional domains.

He will assume office upon the retirement of General Upendra Dwivedi on 30 June 2026.

Read here: pib.gov.in/PressReleasePage.…

He will assume office on 30 June 2026, succeeding Gen Upendra Dwivedi upon his retirement.

An Armoured Corps officer commissioned in 1986, Lt Gen Seth has commanded formations ranging from an Armoured Regiment to the Sudarshan Chakra Corps and has previously led both South Western and Southern Commands.

Sridhar Vembu (@svembu)

This is big: all access to Mythos and Fable AI models disabled for everyone outside America.

First thoughts:

1. Technology is the ultimate weapon. National sovereignty, national security, all of it is now about technology.

2. Globalization is dead and Bharat must find her own way ahead.

We must keep these two ideas in mind.

What can our government do right now? Ensure that orgs in India embrace smaller models, both Indian and Chinese open source ones. With a bit of effort, we can make them work. Anyway, why pay money to people who don't even want to sell to you?

We must deepen our R&D. Sarvam has been on it and we have been on it but remember that the latest models cost not only huge GPU budgets to train, the GPUs themselves are restricted. So we can't afford the scale of money (of the order of $100+ billion to even get in the game!) and even if we could come up with the money, we can't get all the GPUs. I would not like to ask the government to fund tens of billions of dollars on this anyway - the money has far better uses.

Zoho has been pursuing alternative R&D approaches that are far, far less expensive but by its nature cutting edge R&D takes time and we are patient. I am confident we will get there.

Any remaining people in India who have delusions about globalization should wake up now.

tweets.title:
Meghalaya has successfully completed approximately 90% of the border fencing along its international boundary with Bangladesh.

Total Border Length: 443 km
Fencing Completed: 400 km (90%)
Pending Stretch: 40–45 km

The remaining 10% of the project is delayed due to specific logistical and local grievances. In Meghalaya, unlike other states, many villages and farmlands are situated right near the zero line. Constructing a fence 150 yards away from the zero line would split villages and disrupt subsistence agriculture. The state government is currently engaging with local communities to resolve these alignment issues.

BREAKING: India Demonstrates Multi-Layered Ballistic Missile Defence Capability

The DRDO has successfully conducted three consecutive flight tests, demonstrating a multi-layered defence shield against long-range ballistic missile threats and validating a new medium-range naval anti-ship missile.

Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said the tests place India among an elite group of nations capable of intercepting ballistic missiles, including threats in the ICBM class.

The trials successfully validated multiple interceptors and advanced technologies designed to counter evolving missile threats.

The maiden flight test of the Naval Anti-Ship Missile (Medium Range) was also successfully completed, marking another milestone for India's indigenous defence capabilities.
